title: Finding Federal Savings Committee Resolution
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Rules.
plain_summary: This resolution creates a new House committee to find federal programs that are not working well or are not needed. The committee will look at these programs and suggest ways to change or get rid of them. Any laws the committee suggests will get special fast-track treatment in Congress. The committee will stop existing at the end of the 120th Congress.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Rules.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This resolution can be called the Finding Federal Savings Committee Resolution.

sections.title: Section 2 Committee on the Elimination of Nonessential Federal Programs
sections.text: This section creates a new committee in the House of Representatives called the Committee on the Elimination of Nonessential Federal Programs. The committee's job is to change or get rid of federal programs that are not working well or are not needed. The committee will stop existing at the end of the one hundred twentieth Congress. The committee must research and study federal programs that are failing or unnecessary, and decide which ones should be changed or eliminated. The committee must make recommendations to the House about what actions to take regarding these programs. At least once a year, the committee must give the House detailed reports about what they found and a list of programs that are not working or not needed. The committee must also propose new laws to eliminate these programs and suggest budget cuts based on their findings. The committee will have seventeen members total. Four members will come from the Appropriations Committee, four from the Budget Committee, four from the Oversight and Government Reform Committee, and four from the Ways and Means Committee. Two more members who do not serve on any of those four committees will be added. The Speaker of the House will pick one to be the Chair, and the minority leader will pick the other to be the Vice Chair. The Chair and Vice Chair cannot be from the same political party. From each of the four main committees, two of the four members must be from the minority party. When this committee proposes a new law, special rules apply to speed up the process. After seven days not counting days when the House is not meeting, anyone can make a motion to start considering the bill. This motion gets top priority, cannot be debated, cannot be changed, and the vote on it cannot be reconsidered. Debate on the bill is limited to ten hours total, split equally between supporters and opponents. No one can propose changes to the bill or send it back to committee, and the final vote cannot be reconsidered.
